message:
fix(agent-state): allow TERMINATING transition from early lifecycle states

Previously, agents in SPAWNING or INITIALIZING states could only
transition to READY or FAILED. This created issues when trying to
terminate agents during startup - the state machine would reject
the TERMINATING transition as invalid.

Changes to valid transitions:
- SPAWNING: add TERMINATING as valid target (alongside INITIALIZING, FAILED)
- INITIALIZING: add TERMINATING as valid target (alongside READY, FAILED)

Both packages updated for consistency:
- apps/gateway/src/models/agent-state.ts
- packages/shared/src/agent/state.ts

This enables graceful cancellation of agent startup, which is needed for:
- User-initiated cancellation during slow initialization
- Timeout-based termination of stalled spawns
- Cleanup during swarm shutdown

Test coverage added for the new transitions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -51,9 +51,14 @@ export const VALID_TRANSITIONS: Readonly<
 > = {
   [LifecycleState.SPAWNING]: [
     LifecycleState.INITIALIZING,
+    LifecycleState.TERMINATING,
+    LifecycleState.FAILED,
+  ],
+  [LifecycleState.INITIALIZING]: [
+    LifecycleState.READY,
+    LifecycleState.TERMINATING,
     LifecycleState.FAILED,
   ],
-  [LifecycleState.INITIALIZING]: [LifecycleState.READY, LifecycleState.FAILED],
   [LifecycleState.READY]: [
     LifecycleState.EXECUTING,
     LifecycleState.PAUSED,

@@ -63,8 +63,16 @@ export const IDLE_STATES: ReadonlySet<AgentState> = new Set([
 export const STATE_TRANSITIONS: Readonly<
   Record<AgentState, readonly AgentState[]>
 > = {
-  [AgentState.SPAWNING]: [AgentState.INITIALIZING, AgentState.FAILED],
-  [AgentState.INITIALIZING]: [AgentState.READY, AgentState.FAILED],
+  [AgentState.SPAWNING]: [
+    AgentState.INITIALIZING,
+    AgentState.TERMINATING,
+    AgentState.FAILED,
+  ],
+  [AgentState.INITIALIZING]: [
+    AgentState.READY,
+    AgentState.TERMINATING,
+    AgentState.FAILED,
+  ],
   [AgentState.READY]: [
     AgentState.EXECUTING,
     AgentState.PAUSED,
